subject: Mozi Minute: Proof First Business
body:
Words I like: Most businesses ask for reviews after the work is done, but smart businesses build proof creation into the work itself.

Mozi Minute: Proof First Business

Most businesses treat testimonials like an afterthought.

They finish the work, then awkwardly ask "Hey, would you mind leaving us a review?"

By then, the magic moment has passed.

The customer is already thinking about their next problem.

You missed your shot.

Here's the fix: Build proof collection into your service delivery. Don't ask for it later. Capture it during.

Real examples that work:

Gym: Take before/after photos on day 1 and day 30. "This is for your progress tracking." Then ask permission to use the best transformations for marketing.

Consultant: Record a quick video recap after each breakthrough session. "This is for your records." Half become testimonials when you ask later.

Web designer: Screenshot the old site before you start work. Send side-by-side comparisons during the project. "Look at this progress!" Most clients volunteer to share these.

Accountant: Document the tax savings in a simple one-page summary. "Here's what we saved you this year." Those numbers become case studies.

The psychology:

When you're actively documenting their wins, customers feel more value. When they see visual proof of progress, they want to share it.

Pro tip:

Always ask permission to document first.

"Would it be okay if we track your progress visually? It helps us deliver better results."

They'll say yes, and you've pre-sold them on the idea of sharing later.

What to do next:

- Capture "before" on every customer

- Identify your "proof moments" - when customers see clear value

- Build documentation into those moments naturally

- Make the documentation benefit them first (progress tracking, records, etc.)

- Ask permission upfront to capture these moments

- Follow up later with "Can we share your success story?"

This turns every customer into potential marketing material without being pushy.

And you only need to put work into the ones who work out spectacularly.

For everyone else, it's just ammo for upsells later.

As promised, value in under a minute.

Proof >

Alex
